meaning: adj.该咒的,可憎恨的,讨厌的,恶劣的,坏透的
examples: Accusing us of being disloyal to cover his own sorry behaviour is truly execrable.
She always has execrable taste in men.
The execrable pastiche bears no relation to the attitude of most Westerners or their governments to Islam.
Ignoring this reality is a formula for execrable results.
